task_completion.description: Open the 'world_survey' project in the GIS application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GIS application (e.g., QField) open with the 'world_survey' project loaded, visible in the title bar, project list, or layer panel.

task_completion.description: Identify Cairo and view its feature attributes to retrieve the coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the feature info panel or attribute table for 'Cairo' open, displaying its Latitude and Longitude values.

task_completion.description: Select the 'field_observations' layer to prepare for adding a new feature.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the 'field_observations' layer highlighted or selected as the active layer in the layer panel or editing menu.

task_completion.description: Initiate the creation of a new point feature on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the app in digitizing/editing mode, with a crosshair on the map or an 'add feature' button being activated for the 'field_observations' layer.
Screenshots should show the attribute entry form for the newly created point, with 'Cairo_Log' typed into the name or identifier field.

task_completion.description: Enter the retrieved Latitude and Longitude values into the description field of the new feature.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the attribute entry form with the description field containing the coordinates (e.g., 'Lat: 30.0626, Lon: 31.248') before saving.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the GIS application's graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the GIS app (e.g., QField) rather than a terminal emulator running SQLite commands or Python scripts.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually opened the Cairo feature to read the coordinates from the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: There must be at least one screenshot showing the agent viewing the Cairo feature attributes in the app, proving they didn't just guess or hallucinate the coordinates.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use a text editor or external database viewer to bypass the GIS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: All map viewing, layer selection, and feature creation should occur within the primary GIS application interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target city is 'Cairo'. The exact coordinates encoded in the database for Cairo are Lat: 30.0626, Lon: 31.248. The agent must transcribe these specific coordinates (or values within a 0.05 tolerance) into the description field of a new point feature named 'Cairo_Log' in the 'field_observations' layer. The project file is located at /sdcard/Android/data/ch.opengis.qfield/files/world_survey.gpkg.
